purpose: Policy-as-data for Prism (Evaluator) grading rubrics, misconception detection, and mastery updates

common_patterns.name: sign_confusion
indicators: Final answer has wrong sign but correct magnitude
Student applies correct formula but negates result
common_patterns.likely_misconception: Confusion between reference direction and actual direction
common_patterns.remediation: [Sage] Explain reference conventions and sign interpretation

common_patterns.name: unit_dimension_mismatch
indicators: Student mixes unit systems (SI vs. CGS)
Student forgets to convert intermediate results

common_patterns.name: formula_misapplication
indicators: Student uses right formula but wrong variables
Student applies formula outside valid domain
Student confuses similar-looking formulas (e.g., Ohm's law vs. Kirchhoff's current law)
common_patterns.remediation: [Sage] Explain derivation and valid domain; [Scout] Related worked examples

common_patterns.name: algebraic_manipulation_error
indicators: Invalid algebraic move (e.g., canceling across sum)
Incorrect exponent or order of operations
common_patterns.remediation: [Forge] Similar problem for practice; [Prism] Check working again

common_patterns.name: prerequisite_gap
indicators: Student gets stuck on foundational math (e.g., complex numbers, logarithms)
Student doesn't understand notation (e.g., partial derivatives)
Student can't manipulate equations due to missing algebraic skill
common_patterns.remediation: [Pathfinder] Identify prerequisite gap; [Sage] Explain foundational topic; [Forge] Prerequisite practice

detection_algorithm.step_1: Classify error type (sign, unit, formula, algebra, prerequisite)
detection_algorithm.step_4: If prob > 0.65, flag and recommend remediation
detection_algorithm.step_5: If prob < 0.50, mark as 'careless mistake' and suggest re-attempt

prior_model.distribution: Beta(α, β) — conjugate prior for binary (correct/incorrect) outcomes

likelihood_function.correct_with_partial_reasoning: p_correct = 0.85 (indicates understanding but gaps in explanation)
likelihood_function.correct_with_poor_reasoning: p_correct = 0.60 (indicates luck or rote memorization, not true mastery)

update_formula.description: For each attempt, update Beta distribution based on outcome and reasoning quality
update_formula.pseudocode: If correct_with_perfect_reasoning: α += 1.5; If correct_poor_reasoning: α += 0.5; If incorrect: β += 1.5

confidence_interval.interpretation: 95% confident true mastery is between 68–76%; point estimate is 72%

trend_analysis.trending_up: Recent 3 attempts improving; confidence increasing (α growing faster than β)
trend_analysis.trending_down: Recent 3 attempts declining; suggest review or prerequisite work
trend_analysis.stable: Performance consistent; ready to move on or increase difficulty

partial_credit_rules.general_principle: Award points for demonstrating understanding, even if final answer is wrong

examples.situation: Student sets up correct formula but makes arithmetic error
Student derives correct intermediate result but can't finish derivation
examples.credit: 60% (demonstrates understanding of first half, fails on second half)

examples.situation: Student shows correct steps but forgets unit conversion
Student explains concept correctly but makes one misconception
examples.credit: 70% (mostly correct understanding, one systematic error to fix)

grade_structure.headline: Grade: X/10 (or X%)
grade_structure.justification: [1–2 sentence summary of what was correct/wrong]

examples: ✓ Applied Ohm's law correctly to each branch
✓ Set up KVL equation with correct sign conventions
✓ Solved linear system using elimination method
✗ Final answer has wrong sign. You reversed the reference direction in your diagram but didn't update the final interpretation. Misconception: Reference direction ≠ actual direction.
✗ Units are wrong: [current] instead of [voltage]. Misconception: Confused Ohm's law formula.

remediation_path.example: → [Sage] Explain sign conventions | [Forge] 3 practice problems with explicit sign checking | [Pathfinder] Mastery update

confidence_score.example: Confidence in grade: 0.92 (clear error with definable misconception)

grade_below_50_percent.gate_message: Low score detected. Is this a careless mistake or systematic misunderstanding? (Careless / Systematic / Not sure)
grade_below_50_percent.action_if_careless: Suggest re-attempt with fresh eyes; confidence ceiling on mastery update = 0.50
grade_below_50_percent.action_if_systematic: Proceed with misconception detection and remediation

misconception_detected.gate_message: Detected possible misconception: [name]. Offer [Sage] explanation link?
misconception_detected.action_if_confirmed: Link to Sage explanation + offer practice set from Forge

mastery_shift_large.trigger: Posterior mastery differs from prior by > 0.15
mastery_shift_large.gate_message: Mastery estimate changed significantly (was X, now Y). Does this match your self-assessment?

grade_uncertain.trigger: Multiple reasonable interpretations of student's work
grade_uncertain.gate_message: Your work is ambiguous here. Can you explain your reasoning?
grade_uncertain.action_if_clarified: Grade based on clarification; confidence score increases

when_problem_type_unknown.action: Request clarification: 'What type of problem is this? (Calculation / Derivation / Explanation / Other)'

when_rubric_not_defined.action: Apply general rubric (correctness 60% + reasoning 40%); note limitation

when_unclear_if_correct.action: Flag for human review: 'This answer is ambiguous or partially correct. Instructor review recommended.'

log_every: Bayesian update (prior α/β, outcome, posterior α/β, new mastery estimate)
